source.definition: to put something together with an existing amount or collection to increase or complete it

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "add" comes from the Latin word "addere," meaning 'to give to' or 'to put to'.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „add“ provine din cuvântul latin „addere“, care înseamnă „a da către“ sau „a adăuga“.

source.additional: A regular verb commonly used in everyday language, especially in digital and mathematical contexts.

target.definition: a completa sau a aduce ceva la o cantitate sau la un ansamblu existent

explanation.sourceText: The verb derives from the Latin "addere," which means "to add."
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a adăuga“ derivă din latină, de la „addere“, care înseamnă „a adăuga“.

target.additional: Regulary conjugated verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Often used to add something to a quantity, list, or document.

explanation.sourceText: Also used in the sense of attaching something to a document or message.
explanation.targetText: Poate însemna și a atașa ceva unui document sau mesaj.

examples.sourceText: I add the results to the report.

explanation.sourceText: "Added" can also mean becoming part of a group or discussion in a social context.
explanation.targetText: „A adăuga“ poate însemna și a deveni parte dintr-un grup sau discuție.

examples.sourceText: I want to add something to the discussion.
examples.targetText: Vreau să adaug ceva la discuție.

examples.sourceText: They add an overview to the report.
examples.targetText: Ei adaugă un rezumat la raport.

examples.sourceText: He added new information every month.
examples.targetText: El adăuga informații noi în fiecare lună.

examples.sourceText: I will add the new element.
The added material is very useful.

learningTips.text: Think of the English word "add" as putting one thing on top of another, like adding sugar to your coffee to make it sweeter.
Remember that "add" is used a lot in math and technology, just like "a adăuga" is used in Romanian for adding data or files.
The Romanian verb "a adăuga" sounds like "add" with an extra ending, making it easier to recall its meaning.
Imagine adding friends on social media—"to add" someone means to include them, similar to "a adăuga" in Romanian.
